step_by_step_thinking:
Tool Call: retrieve("initial management of single-tooth malocclusions")

Retrieved Documents: 32 documents

Based on the gathered information about initial approaches to addressing single-tooth malocclusions like rotation, I concur that the first action should involve applying localized corrective forces rather than jumping right into invasive procedures. Direct manipulation could potentially realign the tooth efficiently without moving onto fixed orthodontic appliances prematurely (\boxed{C}). The literature suggests clear aligners might offer another option though some restrictions exist regarding how severe a situation they handle well. Furthermore, emerging technologies hint at innovative ways we may refine our current methods even closer to natural movements with reduced discomfort compared to conventional brackets and wires - reinforcing why starting conservatively makes sense until thorough assessments confirm no change needed.
